title: Create table as changes engine to MergeTree if given TTL or settings
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
When creating a table using AS to copy a variant of a MergeTree, with or without CLONE, the specific engine is copied for simple commands, but it reverts to MergeTree if additional arguments are included (e.g. TTL or settings).
[Fiddle examples](https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/848d3bd9-fb42-4a72-bbce-241b3b63c83a)
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE TABLE t1 (a Timestamp) engine=ReplacingMergeTree ORDER BY a;
CREATE TABLE t2 as t1;
CREATE TABLE t3 as t1 settings date_time_output_format='iso';
```
### Expected behavior
The engines of the resulting tables should be consistent, at least when engine is not specified in the create as statement.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (a TIMESTAMP, b INT) AS SELECT a, b FROM (VALUES (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, 1), (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '1 day', 2)) AS temp(a, b);
CREATE TABLE t2 AS SELECT * FROM t1;
CREATE TABLE t3 AS SELECT * FROM t1;
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, 1), (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '1 day', 2);
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE b IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Table engine reverts with TTL or settings in CREATE AS
